title: narcissus × gracilis var. tenuior
intro: Narcissus × gracilis var. tenuior, also known as the narrow-leaved narcissus, is a variety of the daffodil plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a bulbous perennial with narrow, green leaves and trumpet-shaped flowers, typically white or pale yellow in color. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Narcissus × gracilis var. tenuior include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
